---
title: "LRPAL03: Aligned-40-week per-protocol gain for expressive vocabulary (E)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
factor_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "factor_summary.csv")
cohort_marginal = pd.read_csv(_here / "cohort_marginal.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPAL03 is the **aligned-40-week per-protocol** single-gain model for expressive vocabulary (E)
(`eowpvt`). Both arms are aligned by intervention onset across ~two periods
(~the 40-week RLI program): the immediate arm onsets at t1 (aligned window
**t1 -> t3**) and the wait-list arm at t2, post-crossover (aligned window
**t2 -> t4**). One row per child, so there is no child random intercept.

::: {.callout-important title="Per-protocol: NOT a randomised contrast"}

The cohort term (`beta_cohort`, immediate vs wait-list at the aligned endpoints)
is **not** the ITT effect: it is confounded by **age-at-onset** (the wait-list arm
reaches its window ~3-4 months older) and by cohort/timing. Under this design
**no** coefficient is a clean treatment effect -- every term, the cohort contrast
included, is an **association**. The randomised effect of record remains the
LRPITT suite (and its within-person replication LRPDID). Sessions / dose are a
collider and enter only the separate dose sensitivity variant.

:::

## Model

$$
E_{\text{post(aligned)}} \sim \operatorname{BetaBinomial}(n, \operatorname{expit}(\eta), \kappa),\quad
\eta = \alpha + \beta_{\text{cohort}}\,\text{immediate}
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(E_{\text{onset}})
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{onset,std}} + \gamma_{\text{ability}}z(\text{blocks}).
$$

One row per child (no random intercept). Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=24%}
![tau (cohort)](prior_tau.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=24%}

## Prior-predictive check

![Prior predictive](prior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior-predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Findings

### Factor summary (all associations)

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_summary
```

Every `role` is **association** -- the per-protocol design identifies no causal
term. `beta_cohort` is the immediate-vs-wait-list contrast at the aligned
endpoints; `mean`/`lo`/`hi` = posterior mean + equal-tailed 95% CrI;
`prob_positive` = $P(\text{coef} > 0)$.

### Cohort marginal (per-protocol, NOT randomised)

```{python}
# | echo: false
cohort_marginal
```

The items-scale immediate-vs-wait-list difference at the aligned endpoints --
a per-protocol association confounded by age-at-onset and timing, not the
randomised treatment effect.

**Reading these tables.** Per the project's reporting conventions (`METHODS.md`,
"Interpret"), lead with the **interval**, not the point estimate, and read
`prob_positive` / `prob_trt_pos` as evidence of **direction, not magnitude**. At this
design's small per-arm sample (~26–28) a point estimate is on average
magnitude-inflated (a Type-M / winner's-curse effect), so the posterior mean is not a
number to quote on its own. A ROPE-anchored **magnitude** read (`P(|effect| ≥ δ)`
against a minimally-important difference, as now reported for the randomised ITT
suite) is **deliberately deferred** for this per-protocol family — applying a
"meaningful benefit" ROPE to a confounded association would mis-frame it; see
`notes/202606261343-lrpal-aligned-design.md`.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
